---
title: "Transformando com o PySpark"
author: "Angelus Trindade"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
lang: pt
---

Para esse estudo, vamos usar os dados públicos de e-commerce de uma 
loja brasileira.

```{python}
import kagglehub
from pathlib import Path

path = Path(kagglehub.dataset_download("olistbr/brazilian-ecommerce"))

customers_file = path / "olist_customers_dataset.csv"
geolocation_file = path / "olist_geolocation_dataset.csv"
order_items_file = path / "olist_order_items_dataset.csv"
order_payments_file = path / "olist_order_payments_dataset.csv"
order_review_file = path / "olist_order_reviews_dataset.csv"
orders_file = path / "olist_orders_dataset.csv"
products_file = path / "olist_products_dataset.csv"
sellers_file = path / "olist_sellers_dataset.csv"
category_name_file = path / "product_category_name_translation.csv"
```

Agora, vamos carregar o spark - para isso, precisamos de uma sessão.

```{python}
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

spark = (
    SparkSession.builder
    .appName("learning")
    .master("local[*]")
    .getOrCreate()
)

```

E, finalmente, lê-mos um csv. Eu me fiz a pergunta: 
"Qual cidade teve maior volume total de compras?".

```{python}
orders = spark.read.csv(str(orders_file), header=True, inferSchema=True)
customers = spark.read.csv(str(customers_file), header=True, inferSchema=True)
geolocation = spark.read.csv(str(geolocation_file), header=True, inferSchema=True)

customers_with_location = customers.join(
	geolocation, 
	on=(customers.customer_zip_code_prefix == geolocation.geolocation_zip_code_prefix)
)

orders_with_customers_and_location = orders.join(
	customers_with_location,
	on="customer_id"
)

orders_with_customers_and_location.show()

order_frequency = (
	orders_with_customers_and_location
	.groupBy("geolocation_city")
	.count()
	.withColumnRenamed("count", "quantidade")
	.orderBy("quantidade", ascending=False)
)

order_frequency.show()
```
